title: GDPR residency gaps for non-regional Azure services
observation: Challenger review found that Azure Front Door and Entra External ID were treated as EU-resident without an explicit exception path for non-regional service data processing
expected: Requirements should explicitly call out global/non-regional services and require EU Data Boundary validation before architecture proceeds
root_cause: Requirements agent mapped RFQ services but did not cross-reference Microsoft EU Data Boundary documentation for global services
recommendation: Requirements agent should auto-flag any non-regional Azure service (Front Door, Entra, Traffic Manager, etc.) and add an EU Data Boundary validation requirement

title: Front Door EU Data Boundary claim overstated
observation: Architect claimed Front Door Premium with EU-only PoP configuration was EU Data Boundary validated. Challenger correctly identified that Azure Front Door is explicitly excluded from Microsoft EU Data Boundary due to its global anycast model.
expected: Architecture should have flagged Front Door as a compliance exception requiring legal approval, not presented it as validated
root_cause: Architect agent relied on general EU Data Boundary language without checking the specific service exclusion list in Microsoft documentation
recommendation: Architect agent should check Microsoft EU Data Boundary service exclusion list and flag any excluded services as compliance blockers

title: Redis M150 usable capacity overstated
observation: Architect claimed M150 (150 GB) meets 128 GB requirement with 22 GB headroom. Challenger identified that ~20% of Azure Managed Redis memory is reserved for non-cache operations, yielding only ~120 GB usable — below 128 GB.
expected: Redis capacity analysis should account for memory reservation overhead (~20%) when calculating usable cache capacity
root_cause: Architect used raw SKU capacity without accounting for Azure Managed Redis internal memory reservation
recommendation: Architect agent should always apply memory reservation factors when sizing cache services — Azure Managed Redis reserves ~20% for internal operations

title: Scalability math error: 2M/month is 0.77 RPS not 77 RPS
observation: Architecture stated 2M transactions/month equals ~77 requests/second. Correct calculation: 2,000,000 / (30 × 24 × 3600) ≈ 0.77 RPS average
expected: Correct arithmetic with business-hours concentration for realistic peak load estimates
root_cause: Arithmetic error in RPS conversion — likely forgot to divide by 3600 (seconds/hour)
recommendation: Architect agent should use a standard capacity model that converts monthly transactions to peak RPS using business-hours concentration factors

title: APIM Standard v2 does not support Internal VNet injection
observation: Plan configured virtualNetworkType: Internal on APIM Standard v2, but Microsoft docs show Internal VNet injection only applies to Developer/Premium/Premiumv2 tiers. Standard v2 uses VNet integration (outbound) + Private Endpoint (inbound).
expected: Plan should correctly model APIM networking based on the selected SKU tier capabilities
root_cause: Planner applied generic APIM security pattern without checking tier-specific VNet support
recommendation: Planner agent should validate APIM networking model against the selected SKU tier — Standard v2 and Basic v2 have different VNet capabilities than classic tiers

title: PostgreSQL PE vs delegated subnet inconsistency
observation: Module dependency table listed a PE for PostgreSQL while task narrative correctly described delegated subnet networking. Approval gate showed 4 PEs. These internal contradictions create ambiguity for CodeGen.
expected: All sections of the plan should consistently describe the same networking model for each service
root_cause: Copy-paste pattern in module dependency table applied PE to all data services without checking PostgreSQL Flexible Server specifics
recommendation: Planner should cross-check all dependency table entries against task-level networking descriptions before finalizing

title: PostgreSQL password-only auth violates security baseline
observation: CodeGen set activeDirectoryAuth: Disabled and passwordAuth: Enabled. Security baseline requires AAD-only auth for all databases.
expected: AAD auth enabled, password auth disabled by default for production
root_cause: CodeGen defaulted to password-only auth without checking security baseline requirements
recommendation: CodeGen should always set activeDirectoryAuth: Enabled as the default for PostgreSQL Flexible Server

title: APIM Standard v2 used Classic VNet injection model (virtualNetworkType: External)
observation: CodeGen used virtualNetworkConfiguration + virtualNetworkType: External, which is the classic model for Developer/Premium tiers. Standard v2 requires virtualNetworkIntegration + PE.
expected: CodeGen should use correct VNet model based on APIM SKU tier
root_cause: Used raw resource instead of AVM module, which correctly surfaces the right VNet model per SKU
recommendation: Always use AVM modules for APIM to get correct tier-specific property model. Never use raw resources when AVM is available.

title: Front Door privateLinkLocation inherited profile location (global) instead of resource region
observation: Front Door profile location is 'global' but Private Link origin needs actual region of target resource (swedencentral). privateLinkLocation inherited 'global' from location param.
expected: Separate parameter for target resource region vs Front Door profile location
root_cause: Single location param used for both Front Door profile (global) and Private Link target (regional)
recommendation: Front Door modules should always use separate params: location (for profile) and resourceLocation (for Private Link targets)
